data.title: Orrum partners with LivaNova to integrate CŌRE Insights™ analytics platform with Essenz Perfusion System.
data.description: Orrum Clinical Analytics has revealed that their CŌRE Insights™ analytics platform is set to integrate seamlessly with LivaNova's Essenz Perfusion System. This collaboration, announced on Feb. 20, 2026, marks a significant strategic partnership between Orrum Clinical Analytics and LivaNova USA Inc. The integration promises to streamline processes and enhance efficiency for healthcare providers.

data.title: Home Instead awards 10 Care Pros of the Year for commitment to helping older adults age at home with dignity.
data.description: Home Instead, part of Honor, a leading provider of in-home care for elderly individuals, revealed the recipients of its highly esteemed Care Pro of the Year Award. Ten exceptional care professionals from various regions in the United States and Canada were recognized for their dedication to ensuring older adults maintain dignity, independence, and happiness at home. This annual award highlights Home Instead’s acknowledgment of outstanding commitment within the caregiving sector.

data.title: Domino's Pizza (NASDAQ: DPZ) Q4 earnings to be $5.38 per share, revenue $1.52 billion. Potential $500/month from stock dividends.
data.description: Domino's Pizza, Inc. is set to announce Q4 earnings before the market opens on Monday, Feb. 23. Analysts predict earnings of $5.38 per share, surpassing last year's $4.89. Projections for quarterly revenue stand at $1.52 billion, up from $1.44 billion last year. BTIG's Peter Saleh maintains a Buy rating for Domino's, adjusting the price target to $500 from $530. Investors are also eyeing dividends, with Domino's offering an annual yield of 1.81%, equating to $1.74 per share quarterly ($6.96 annually). For those aiming to earn $500 monthly from Domino's, the yearly goal is $6,000.

data.title: Capitolis acquires 20 Gates Management's U.S. Secured Financing Platform to expand its Capital Marketplace and clients base.
data.description: A financial technology company, Capitolis, recently revealed its acquisition of 20 Gates Management’s U.S. Secured Financing Platform. This agreement, slated to finalize in the upcoming weeks, will allow Capitolis to incorporate U.S. secured financing into its Capital Marketplace. This move aims to diversify its clientele and bring experienced professionals from 20 Gates Management to Capitolis. The U.S. Secured Financing Platform by 20 Gates Management has been in operation for over 15 years, showcasing a strong track record in the industry. This strategic acquisition sets the stage for Capitolis to expand its offerings and strengthen its position in the market.

data.title: Upstart sells $333 million in Upstart Auto assets to affiliates of Bayview Asset Management, LLC, praising strategic fit.
data.description: Upstart Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: UPST), a prominent AI lending marketplace based in San Mateo, California, disclosed the successful sale of a portfolio worth $333 million of Upstart Auto assets to affiliates of Bayview Asset Management, LLC. Carlos Prevolis, Managing Director at Bayview, emphasized the alignment between Upstart and Bayview's commitment to prudent capital utilization in the auto lending sector. Prevolis noted admiration for Upstart's expansion and...

data.title: Telecom leader A.J. Moul becomes GeoLinks' Chief Commercial Officer to lead sales, marketing. Excited to join.
data.description: In Camarillo, California, GeoLinks, a prominent company in next-generation fixed wireless services and the top holder of licensed Local Multipoint Distribution Service (LMDS) spectrum in the US, has named A.J. Moul as Chief Commercial Officer. With extensive experience in the telecommunications sector, Moul will oversee sales, marketing, and product activities as a key member of the executive team, contributing to the company's strategic vision. This move signals GeoLinks' commitment to growth and innovation in the industry.

data.title: MARA plans conference call for Q4 and FY2025 financial results.
data.description: A new report reveals that there has been a significant increase in the number of COVID-19 cases in the past week. Over 10,000 new cases have been reported, bringing the total number of cases to more than 50,000. Additionally, the number of deaths has risen to over 500. This spike in cases has raised concerns among health officials, who urge the public to follow safety guidelines and protocols to prevent further spread of the virus. The government is considering implementing stricter measures to curb the growing number of infections.

data.title: Dine Brands Global, Inc. will pay $0.19 dividend per share to stockholders; payable on April 10, 2026.
data.description: Dine Brands Global, Inc., the parent company of Applebee's Neighborhood Grill + Bar, IHOP, and Fuzzy’s Taco Shop restaurants, declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.19 per share of common stock. This dividend will be paid on April 10, 2026, to stockholders of record as of March 18, 2026. Headquartered in Pasadena, California, Dine Brands Global, Inc. operates under the NYSE ticker symbol DIN.

data.title: Zenocutuzumab treatment shows continued benefit in NRG1+ NSCLC patients beyond progression: eNRGy trial results.
data.description: In Lexington, Massachusetts on February 20, 2026, Partner Therapeutics, Inc. (PTx) shared results of a supplementary examination of the eNRGy trial (NCT02912949) focusing on extending treatment with zenocutuzumab in patients with advanced neuregulin 1 fusion-positive (NRG1+) non-small cell cancer. The study delved into the efficacy of this approach in cases where the disease continued to progress. This analysis sheds light on the potential benefits of zenocutuzumab beyond the standard treatment endpoints. The trial encompassed a significant number of participants, highlighting the potential impact of this innovative treatment approach on patients with this specific type of cancer.

data.title: Scale Computing announces agenda and speaker lineup for Platform//2026 Summit in Austin, Texas on Feb 20, 2026.
data.description: Austin, Texas - On February 20, 2026, Scale Computing revealed the schedule for Platform//2026™, its premier event catering to Innovative Leaders, Technology Partners, and Service providers. As a top player in edge computing and network solutions, Scale Computing is set to host the summit for key industry figures.

data.title: New gated community in Pico Rivera offers townhomes from mid $700,000s, walkable to schools/parks. KB Home project.
data.description: In Pico Rivera, California, Ventana presents custom-designed townhomes featuring upscale amenities and close proximity to schools and parks. These homes are priced starting from the mid $700,000s. KB Home, a prominent U.S. homebuilder listed on NYSE as KBH, is overseeing this development. Residents can expect resort-style facilities and a convenient location catered to their needs.

data.title: Ramaco Resources to disclose Q4 and full-year 2025 financials on Feb 25, 2026, followed by conference call on Feb 26.
data.description: In Lexington, Kentucky, on February 20, 2026, Ramaco Resources, Inc. (NASDAQ: METC, METCB) announced plans to disclose its financial performance for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025 on Wednesday, February 25, 2026, post-market closure. The company, known as Ramaco, will provide details through an earnings news release.

data.title: U.S. Navy approves Raytheon's StormBreaker smart weapon for Super Hornets, enhancing fleet capabilities. Raytheon is owned by RTX (NYSE: RTX).
data.description: The United States Navy recently authorized the operational deployment of Raytheon's StormBreaker® smart weapon on the F/A-18-E/F Super Hornet strike fighter, enhancing the fleet's capacity for precise strikes. This approval signifies an important step towards bolstering fleet effectiveness and protection. Raytheon, a subsidiary of RTX (NYSE: RTX), developed this advanced weapon system, showcasing the Navy's commitment to modernizing its capabilities.

data.title: FDA approves AbbVie/AstraZeneca combo treatment for slow-growth blood cancer CLL, enhancing care standard; 77% patients progression-free at 3 years.
data.description: The FDA recently approved AbbVie Inc.'s combination treatment of Venclexta and AstraZeneca Plc.'s Calquence for chronic lymphocytic leukemia. This marks the first all-oral, fixed-duration treatment for untreated CLL patients. AbbVie and Roche Holdings AG developed Venclexta, aiming to enhance the standard of care for CLL patients. The approval is significant given CLL's commonality in adults, with the new regimen set to improve patient outcomes. The decision was based on positive results from the AMPLIFY Phase 3 trial, indicating 77% of patients treated with the combination remained progression-free at three years. This development offers a targeted treatment option with potential time off treatment for patients, as reported by Benzinga.com.

data.title: NEXE Innovations sent out 315K compostable coffee pods this year, expanded to 20 SKUs (+8), boosting orders and distribution.
data.description: So far this year, NEXE has sent out 315,000 compostable pods that are BPI-certified. They have also increased their product offerings to 20 different varieties, adding 8 new SKUs. This expansion has led to an increase in repeat orders and wider distribution in Office Coffee Service (OCS) channels.

data.title: FTC Solar (Nasdaq: FTCI) to reveal Q4 and full year 2025 financial results on March 5, 2026; based in Austin, Texas.
data.description: In Austin, Texas, on February 20, 2026, FTC Solar, Inc. (Nasdaq: FTCI), renowned for its solar tracker systems, software, and engineering services, disclosed its plan to unveil the financial outcomes for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025. The report is scheduled to be released before the market opens on Thursday, March 5, 2026.

data.title: Metaplanet CEO refutes criticism, defends company's performance compared to Bitcoin, cites 23% stock decline versus BTC's 24% drop. CEO emphasizes transparency with live dashboard for shareholders.
data.description: Metaplanet's CEO, Simon Gerovich, has responded to allegations of lack of transparency and mismanagement of Bitcoin purchases, citing a 23% stock decline compared to Bitcoin's 24% drop as evidence of their strategy's success. Gerovich defended the company against claims of dishonest disclosures and hiding high-priced Bitcoin purchases, calling the accusations factually incorrect. He emphasized his willingness to take responsibility for all actions and statements made by Metaplanet. Gerovich highlighted that all Bitcoin addresses are publicly accessible on a live dashboard for shareholders to monitor holdings in real time, ensuring transparency. For more details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
